meta.title: Seedance 2.0 — Next-Gen Multimodal Video Model | OpenCreator
meta.description: Seedance 2.0 supports four-modal input — images, videos, audio, and text — for cinematic video generation with precise reference control, smooth motion, and storytelling continuity.

hero.description: Seedance 2.0 accepts four types of input — images, videos, audio, and text — enabling cinematic generation with precise reference control. Set visual style with an image, define motion with a video clip, set mood with audio, and direct it all through natural language.

showcase.title: See It in Action
showcase.subtitle: From choreography to action sequences — explore what Seedance 2.0 can generate across different creative scenarios.

streetDance.title: Street Dance Battle
streetDance.useCase: High-tempo movement with beat-aware cuts and character continuity under fast choreography.

relationshipDrama.title: Domestic Drama
relationshipDrama.useCase: Emotion-driven scene pacing with close-up performance and natural conversational timing.

musicMV.title: Music Video
musicMV.useCase: Stylized visual motifs synchronized with music cadence and atmosphere shifts.

wuxiaDuel.title: Wuxia Duel
wuxiaDuel.useCase: Complex martial arts movement, directional camera transitions, and dramatic fight choreography.

monsterInvasion.title: Monster Invasion
monsterInvasion.useCase: Wide-scene composition, destruction sequences, and continuity in effects-heavy narrative.

cinematicTransition.title: Cinematic Transition
cinematicTransition.useCase: Smooth scene transitions, creative visual effects, and narrative continuity across shots.

workflow.title: Director-Level Workflow
workflow.subtitle: Choose your entry mode, bind references with @, and iterate without starting over — all on one canvas.

entry.title: Choose your entry mode
entry.description: Use First & Last Frames for anchor-driven shots, or All-in-One Reference for multimodal composition.

assets.title: Prioritize high-impact assets
assets.description: Upload assets that define composition, motion rhythm, and tone first — then add secondary references.

addressing.title: Bind materials with @
addressing.description: Assign clear roles — first frame, camera language, character identity, BGM — to each material in your prompt.

editing.title: Continue & iterate
editing.description: Use continuation and timeline-style edits to preserve narrative momentum across versions.

firstLast.title: First & Last Frames
firstLast.description: Best for anchor-frame generation with structured opening and ending control.

allInOne.title: All-in-One Reference
allInOne.description: Designed for multimodal mixing and precise asset orchestration in a single prompt.

promptBlueprint.title: Prompt blueprint

lines.0: @Image 1 as first frame, @Video 1 for camera movement.
lines.1: Reference motion cadence from @Video 2, style from @Image 2.
lines.2: Use @Audio 1 as BGM, align transitions to beat drops.
lines.3: Extend @Video 1 by 5s, end with a clean close-up.

promptBlueprint.note: Label each @ reference explicitly and stay within the mixed-input limits for best results.

workflow.audioHint: No separate audio file? Referenced video audio tracks can still guide rhythm and ambience.

features.title: Core Capabilities
features.subtitle: A comprehensive upgrade in generation quality, controllability, and creative expression.

textImageVideo.title: Four-modal input
textImageVideo.description: Combine text, images, videos, and audio in a single generation — each modality enriches the final output.

multimodalReference.title: Reference-driven control
multimodalReference.description: Upload reference images for composition, videos for camera language and motion, and audio for rhythm — the model understands and reproduces them.

motionConsistency.title: Motion & consistency upgrade
motionConsistency.description: Significantly smoother physical dynamics, more stable movement, and stronger scene-level visual coherence across frames.

continuation.title: Continuation & storytelling
continuation.description: Extend existing footage and build multi-shot narrative sequences — pick up where the last clip left off.

editableWorkflow.title: Generation meets editing
editableWorkflow.description: Support for character replacement, clip insertion, and timeline-style iteration — create and refine in the same workflow.

storytelling.title: Expressive short-form video
storytelling.description: Optimized for choreography, emotional dialogue, music videos, action scenes, and VFX-driven micro stories.

specs.title: Technical Specifications
specs.subtitle: Input limits, generation parameters, and interaction controls at a glance.

values.mixedFiles: Up to 12 files across all modalities
values.outputFormat: MP4 with optional built-in sound effects / BGM
values.timelineEditing: Add, replace, and delete clips within a sequence
values.motionStability: Enhanced physical dynamics and smoother continuous motion
values.characterConsistency: Stronger facial, clothing, and scene-level coherence
values.audioVisualSync: More realistic sound timing, effects, and ambient audio

pricing.title: Coming Soon to OpenCreator
pricing.subtitle: Seedance 2.0 is not yet available — join the waitlist to get notified at launch
pricing.description: Next-gen multimodal video generation by ByteDance
pricing.note: Pricing and availability will be announced after integration and quality validation are complete.

0.question: What is Seedance 2.0?
0.answer: Seedance 2.0 is ByteDance's next-generation video model that supports four-modal input — images, videos, audio, and text — for controllable, cinematic video generation.

1.question: When will Seedance 2.0 be available on OpenCreator?
1.answer: We're actively working on integration. Join the waitlist to be the first to know when it launches.

2.question: What are the input limits?
2.answer: Up to 9 images, 3 video clips (≤ 15s total), 3 audio files (≤ 15s total), and a combined maximum of 12 mixed references.

3.question: Does it support video continuation and editing?
3.answer: Yes. Seedance 2.0 supports extending existing footage, inserting new scenes between clips, and replacing characters or segments — all driven by natural language prompts.

4.question: How does the @ reference system work?
4.answer: You assign roles to uploaded materials using @ mentions in your prompt — for example, '@Image 1 as first frame, @Video 1 for camera movement, @Audio 1 as BGM'.

5.question: What types of content is Seedance 2.0 best for?
5.answer: It excels at choreography, emotional short drama, music videos, action sequences, commercial clips, and VFX-driven narrative content with strong reference control.
